title: Copy flatpak's approach to trusting host CA certs
body:
See https://github.com/flatpak/flatpak/pull/1757

We should debate whether it's opt-in (`--host-ca-certs`) or opt-out (`--disable-host-ca-certs`).  The argument for having this opt in is theoretically it's an information leak from the host that some people may not want.  But the argument for having it opt out is...I think almost every "normal" use of podman would want this.

comments.body:
@cgwalters What path would that be
 --host-cert /etc/pki?
@cgwalters ???
@cgwalters Is this still something you suggest we do or should I close?

Why wouldn't we want this?  I mean today I provision the RH internal CA cert on both my host and in my toolbox container, and it's an annoyance every time I spin up a non-toolbox podman container and it can't talk `https://` to internal websites.

I am asking more of what you expect to have happen.
Mount /run/user/%d/p11-kit/pkcs11 into the container?
This will be blocked by SELinux, so we would need to allow this.
Copy and mount "/etc/pkcs11/modules/p11-kit-trust.module" into the container?
Create container specific version of /etc/pkcs11/pkcs11.conf and mount it in place.

Others?

> I am asking more of what you expect to have happen.

The high level is: Define an API for a container to gain access to the host's trusted CA roots.  You're asking more about the implementation:

> Mount /run/user/%d/p11-kit/pkcs11 into the container? This will be blocked by SELinux, so we would need to allow this.

Right, doing it the same way flatpak does would make sense to me.  (It works for flatpak without policy changes because flatpak doesn't apply special labels to its containers)
@vrothberg could you look into this?

> @vrothberg could you look into this?

Sure. I will go through my backlog after Flock.

More and more things are coming on my plate. I'll unassign me, so others could take over.
This issue had no activity for 30 days. In the absence of activity or the "do-not-close" label, the issue will be automatically closed within 7 days.

> We should debate whether it's opt-in (`--host-ca-certs`) or opt-out (`--disable-host-ca-certs`). The argument for having this opt in is theoretically it's an information leak from the host that some people may not want.

… and it is injecting new roots of trust into the container that the container may not expect / want. (That’s not really a security issue, because the host can always subvert the security of the container, but it can be surprising/undesirable for CaaS platforms.)

I do agree that it is a pretty useful feature to have.
@mtrmac @vrothberg Any movement on this?
No, I think it slipped through

@rajibmitra any progress?

Hey, I came across this issue and would like to try working on this although it may take some time I'm a bit slow, I'm also not well versed with CA certs etc. I just have some questions

I checked the path, and it exists, so what is the end goal, are we trying to add opt-in (--host-ca-certs) or opt-out (--disable-host-ca-certs) to the `podman run` command like this would mount it inside the container

This would be for non-root?
```bash
ls -la /run/user/1000/p11-kit/pkcs11
srw------- 1 leon users 0 Mar 10 19:44 /run/user/1000/p11-kit/pkcs11
```
If not me someone else would also get an idea, I'll have to go through the code too not sure where to begin with this its just a bit confusing any pointers would be appreciated too.

I would also like to point out how minikube handles this issue:
https://minikube.sigs.k8s.io/docs/handbook/untrusted_certs/
This way we do not need to install test root certificates on our workstations, or root permissions are not needed.

Is this still on the roadmap ?
I think doing something like minikube would be a nice addition.
But actually my use case is more in relation with [toolbox](https://github.com/containers/toolbox/issues/626) so I don't need to always add private root CA manually...

I am finally about to land the code to address this for Toolbx containers.  I need to write tests, do some more testing, etc..

Some references:
https://github.com/containers/toolbox/issues/626
https://github.com/containers/toolbox/pull/1634
https://github.com/containers/toolbox/pull/1643
https://github.com/containers/toolbox/pull/1644
